cat: cat (noun): feline mammal usually having thick soft fur and no ability to roar; domestic cats belong to family Felidae.
dog: dog (noun): domesticated canid, Canis familiaris, descended from wolves; family Canidae.
feline: feline (noun): any of various lithe-bodied roundheaded fissiped mammals, many with retractile claws.
canine: canine (noun): any of various fissiped mammals with nonretractile claws and typically long muzzles.
felidae: Felidae (noun): a family of mammals including cats, lions, tigers, and related felines.
canidae: Canidae (noun): a family of carnivorous mammals including dogs, wolves, foxes, and jackals.
mammal: mammal (noun): any warm-blooded vertebrate having skin more or less covered with hair; females nourish young with milk.
animal: animal (noun): a living organism characterized by voluntary movement.
bird: bird (noun): warm-blooded egg-laying vertebrates characterized by feathers and forelimbs modified as wings.
fish: fish (noun): any of various mostly cold-blooded aquatic vertebrates usually having scales and breathing through gills.
reptile: reptile (noun): any cold-blooded vertebrate of the class Reptilia.
amphibian: amphibian (noun): cold-blooded vertebrate typically living on land but breeding in water.
earth: Earth (noun): the 3rd planet from the sun; the planet we live on.
sun: Sun (noun): the star that is the source of light and heat for the planets in the solar system.
moon: Moon (noun): the natural satellite of the Earth.
planet: planet (noun): any celestial body (other than comets or satellites) that revolves around a star.
solar system: solar system (noun): the sun with the celestial bodies that revolve around it in its gravitational field.
mercury: Mercury (noun): the smallest planet and the nearest to the sun.
venus: Venus (noun): the second planet from the sun; has a thick atmosphere.
mars: Mars (noun): a small reddish planet that is the 4th from the sun.
jupiter: Jupiter (noun): the largest planet and the 5th from the sun.
saturn: Saturn (noun): a giant planet with an extensive system of rings; 6th from the sun.
uranus: Uranus (noun): a giant planet with a blue-green color; 7th from the sun.
neptune: Neptune (noun): a giant planet with strong atmospheric winds; 8th from the sun.
water: water (noun): binary compound that occurs at room temperature as a clear colorless odorless tasteless liquid; H2O.
oxygen: oxygen (noun): a nonmetallic bivalent element that is normally a colorless odorless tasteless nonflammable diatomic gas; atomic number 8.
hydrogen: hydrogen (noun): a nonmetallic univalent element that is normally a colorless and odorless highly flammable diatomic gas; atomic number 1.
carbon: carbon (noun): an abundant nonmetallic element occurring in all organic compounds; atomic number 6.
carbon dioxide: carbon dioxide (noun): a colorless odorless gas produced by burning carbon and organic compounds and by respiration; CO2.
photosynthesis: photosynthesis (noun): synthesis of compounds with the aid of radiant energy (especially in plants).
gravity: gravity (noun): (physics) the force of attraction between all masses in the universe.
newton: Newton (noun): English mathematician and physicist; formulated laws of motion and universal gravitation.
einstein: Einstein (noun): physicist who developed the special and general theories of relativity.
dna: DNA (noun): (biochemistry) deoxyribonucleic acid; the genetic material of living organisms.
cell: cell (noun): the basic structural and functional unit of all organisms.
cpu: CPU (noun): the part of a computer that performs most of the data processing.
ram: RAM (noun): volatile memory used for temporary storage by running programs.
internet: Internet (noun): a computer network consisting of a worldwide network of computer networks that use the TCP/IP protocols.
http: HTTP (noun): protocol used by the World Wide Web for transmitting hypermedia documents.
https: HTTPS (noun): secure form of HTTP that uses TLS encryption.
json: JSON (noun): text-based data-interchange format derived from JavaScript object notation.
onnx: ONNX (noun): open neural network exchange format for interoperable machine learning model representation.
android: Android (noun): mobile operating system based on Linux, developed by Google.
java: Java (noun): object-oriented programming language designed to be portable.
python: Python (noun): high-level interpreted programming language emphasizing readability.
artificial intelligence: artificial intelligence (noun): the branch of computer science concerned with building systems that perform tasks requiring human intelligence.
machine learning: machine learning (noun): a branch of AI focused on algorithms that learn patterns from data.
large language model: large language model (noun): a neural language model trained on large text corpora to predict and generate text.
rag: retrieval-augmented generation (noun): technique where external retrieved context is supplied to a language model before answering.
danexchat: DanexChat (proper noun): on-device assistant app built on SmolLM by DanexCodr (Danison Nuñez).
